core_mechanism: threshold-gated discrete single-quantum transfer onto a small isolated island — transfer is blocked below a fixed charging-energy threshold and only driven above it by an external periodic drive, producing dissipative loss (Coulomb-blockade-type gating)
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the newly identified loss to Coulomb blockade and microwave-driven charge tunnelling in small metallic grains, i.e. single-charge transfer onto a confined island that is energetically suppressed below a charging threshold and activated by the microwave drive.
